sourceText: It shows an attitude that elevated academic value by describing various civilizations with an objective and inclusive perspective, unbound by religion or race.
Al-Battani had to challenge the authoritative theories of the ancient Greek master Ptolemy, dedicating his life to achieving greater accuracy amidst the technological limitations of his era.

Through decades of relentless observation and innovative mathematical methods, he corrected Ptolemy's errors and calculated far more precise values for the solar year and the precession of the equinoxes.
The celestial bodies move in accordance with a divine order; precise observation is the first step to understanding it.
This emphasizes the importance of empirical evidence and meticulous data collection over theory or belief. True knowledge comes from direct observation.
Even the greatest authorities of the past are not infallible. Truth is found not in blind acceptance, but in rigorous verification.
This reflects the belief that scientific inquiry requires a willingness to question established knowledge, constantly testing and refining it.
Mathematics is the language with which God has written the universe. Through it, we can read the heavens.
This highlights the fundamental role of mathematics in understanding and describing the physical phenomena of the universe. The order of the cosmos can be deciphered through mathematical principles.
I was born into poverty and faced lifelong social prejudice and mockery due to my distinct physical appearance, which earned me the nickname 'the goggle-eyed'.

I overcame these adversities through innate intelligence, sharp wit, and prolific writing. My knowledge earned me the patronage of caliphs and patrons, ultimately establishing me as the foremost intellectual of my era.
A book is a master who is silent when you need it, and speaks when you desire it.
This means that a book is the ultimate source of knowledge, providing wisdom at the reader's command without being intrusive, and is always accessible.
